body.title: [in_progress] Skills read the filesystem memory store, not the Postgres palace (context-load + audit/daily)
body.intention: I intend that no skill reads memory from the retired Postgres palace — they read the canonical filesystem store.
body.description:
**Problem:** the canonical-memory consolidation moved memory to the filesystem store (devlab/runtime/memory/), but ~14 skills still `psql` the old `memory_palace` / `adc.palace` tables (and read `.dsb` logs). context-load Steps 2a-3 (rules, palace tree, ADC snapshot, decisions) ARE the old Postgres palace; day-close writes palace.days.* via psql; audit-audits/hypothesis/smell, eval-run, outcome, weekly-retro all query the palace. These return empty or error post-consolidation. This is a MECHANISM rewrite, not the path-token swap (that's T-day-close-audit-skill-stale-paths). **Shape:** rewrite each palace-DB read to read the filesystem store — slate via slate_store, decisions via grep devlab/runtime/memory/decisions/*.json, tickets via the store, rules wherever they now live. context-load is the priority (it's the post-compact entry point and the one that bit us today). **Affected files (repo skills/):** context-load(+/run), day-close, audit-audits, audit-hypothesis, audit-smell, eval-run, outcome, weekly-retro. **Design rules:** filesystem memory store is canonical (D-canonical-memory-consolidation / D-filesystem-memory-store); NO new ~/.unseen_university paths without Akien. **Scope boundary:** IN — survivor skills' palace-DB reads -> filesystem store. OUT — skills slated for retirement (goal/question/concept/audit-goal etc.); the mechanical path-token sweep (separate ticket). **Test plan:** context-load runs end-to-end with ZERO psql calls and surfaces slate + recent decisions from the filesystem store (red: it errors/empties on palace psql today); `grep -rlE 'memory_palace|adc.palace' skills/` returns only retired-and-deleted skills. **Completion criteria:** the 8 survivor skills read the filesystem store; context-load post-compact orientation works without the palace DB. **NOTE (L):** advisor() before sprint.

## 2026-06-24 — context-load slice SPLIT OUT + done
context-load carved into T-context-load-fs-store (closed proven). It turned out the run executor was already fs-store-based; the real bugs were UU_ROOT defaulting to cwd (silently dropped Steps 2a/2b/3 off-root) + stale SKILL.md psql docs. REMAINING here (7 survivor skills): day-close (palace.days write), audit-audits, audit-hypothesis, audit-smell, eval-run, outcome, weekly-retro. Also surfaced a pre-existing failure: T-context-load-violation-summary-missing.

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] datacenter_logs/<device>/<subsystem>/ is the log hierarchy. Never write to a flat — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] PATTERN-001: BaseDevice / BaseShim Lifecycle: `rollback()` must be idempotent — safe to call even if — applies: tags: pattern, pattern-001 (source: docs/design_patterns_inventory.md#PATTERN-001: BaseDevice / BaseShim Lifecycle)
…and 38 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set
